text:
🔭 Agentic SDLC role changes

Senior engineers are becoming agent orchestrators; junior developer hiring has collapsed (~67% fewer entry-level dev postings since 2022). QA is not eliminated — humans define quality objectives, agents run tests. Spec-Driven Development (SDD) is the emerging canonical response to agentic drift: write a formal spec first, generate code from it.

Key points:
• Delegation gap: devs use AI in ~60% of work but can fully delegate only 0–20% of tasks (Anthropic 2026 report; Rakuten: 12.5M-line codebase, 7-hour agent run)
• Junior pipeline crisis: Microsoft CACM (Russinovich & Hanselman, Apr 2026) — AI "drag" on early-career devs; Harvard data: junior employment at AI-adopting firms down 7.7% in 6 quarters
• SDD = specs as source of truth, code generated from them. AWS Kiro (EARS notation, May 2026) is the canonical platform; same pattern in Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Spec Kit
• Hardest unsolved problem: verification gap — agents produce code faster than teams can review; ~70% of hidden defects invisible in diff view; agents mask bugs (sleep-for-race-condition)
• Agile rituals: sprint → task-level; standups auto-generated from GitHub/JIRA; planning reframed as "intent design" (AWS guidance)
• No practitioner-consensus book yet — closest: Anthropic 2026 Trends Report + Russinovich/Hanselman CACM paper
